title: Design Seams the Tests Can Trust - Quick Check

questions.question: What problem does an owned seam solve?
questions.answer: It stops business logic and tests from depending directly on volatile third-party detail by giving the application its own collaborator interface.

questions.question: Why is dependency injection useful at a boundary?
questions.answer: It lets the code receive a collaborator cleanly so tests can control it without deep patching or construction tricks.

questions.question: What still needs verification after adding a seam?
questions.answer: The adapter or translation layer still needs a realistic check to prove it speaks correctly to the real boundary.

questions.question: Where is a seam often best placed?

options.A: At random so more classes exist
options.B: Where foreign detail becomes domain intent

questions.explanation: The course emphasizes seams at the boundary where business meaning emerges from external detail.

questions.question: Which statement best matches the course?

options.A: Seams remove the need for integration coverage
options.B: Seams improve unit clarity but do not erase boundary verification needs
options.C: Seams matter only in Java-like languages
options.D: Seams are just another name for mocks

questions.explanation: The course separates domain clarity from adapter realism rather than pretending one replaces the other.
